headline: Anthropic's $1.5B copyright settlement faces court delays amid author disputes
body: A federal judge has delayed approval of Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement as authors push back against proposed payout terms. The dispute centers on how settlement funds should be distributed among affected content creators whose work trained Claude models. For healthcare AI vendors and implementers, this signals ongoing legal uncertainty around LLM training data that could impact model licensing costs, vendor selection criteria, and compliance obligations for systems deployed in clinical settings. GitLab's expanded integration with Claude models for code generation shows continued enterprise momentum despite these legal headwinds. Healthcare organizations evaluating AI copilots for EHR workflows should monitor how this settlement resolves, as it may affect future licensing structures and vendor liability exposure.
